name: Turn and Burn Motors
description_short: Turn & Burn Motors is a family-owned Buy Here Pay Here used car dealership with two Georgia locations, offering in-house financing to credit-challenged buyers.
description_long:
Turn & Burn Motors is a family-owned, in-house financing used car dealership headquartered in Conyers, Georgia, with a second location in Griffin, GA. The company markets itself as a credit-friendly alternative to traditional auto financing, emphasizing that it cares about customers rather than credit scores. It has been recognized for community involvement and customer service, citing an award on its website and strong Google reviews.

The dealership offers Buy Here Pay Here (BHPH) financing, meaning it acts as both the seller and the lender. Customers browse a rotating inventory of used cars, trucks, and SUVs, submit a short pre-qualification application online, and complete the purchase and financing paperwork on-site. Additional services include a car rental program starting at $55/day, an online payment portal, a trade-in and car-buying program (they purchase vehicles regardless of whether you buy from them), and a referral program. All qualified vehicles come with a 24-month or 24,000-mile limited warranty.

What distinguishes Turn & Burn Motors from typical used car lots is its explicit focus on buyers with damaged or no credit. Unlike banks or credit unions, the dealership makes its own financing decisions in-house, removing the traditional credit score barrier. The two-location footprint, in-house warranty, and car rental service give it more infrastructure than a typical independent BHPH lot. The online pre-qualification form and payment portal add a layer of convenience.

Honestly, this is a BHPH dealership, not a personal loan lender — it belongs in a vehicle financing or auto loans category that CreditDoc does not currently maintain. Consumers should be aware that BHPH financing universally carries above-market interest rates and strict payment terms; APR and loan details are not disclosed on the website. Buyers with poor credit who need reliable transportation and are willing to pay a premium for in-house approval will find Turn & Burn a workable option, but they should compare total cost of financing before committing.
pros: In-house Buy Here Pay Here financing — credit score is not the primary approval factor
24-month or 24,000-mile limited warranty on all qualified vehicles
Two Georgia locations (Conyers and Griffin) for broader regional access
Will purchase your vehicle even if you do not buy from them
Car rental program available starting at $55/day
Convenient online payment portal for existing customers
Family-owned with recognized community involvement and Google-reviewed customer service
cons: No APR, interest rate, or financing terms disclosed on the website — BHPH typically carries very high rates
Inventory consists of high-mileage used vehicles (examples shown: 74K–158K miles)
Limited to two locations in central Georgia — not available outside the region
No mention of reporting payments to credit bureaus, which limits credit-building value
Vehicle selection and financing terms are bundled — you cannot use their financing for a car purchased elsewhere
best_for: Georgia residents with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify at traditional dealerships
Buyers who need a vehicle quickly and want same-visit approval
People who want a warranty included with a used car purchase
Drivers who need a short-term rental while arranging a purchase
diagnosis: Turn & Burn Motors is best for credit-challenged buyers in the Conyers or Griffin, Georgia area who need a vehicle and cannot secure financing through a bank or credit union. The main caveat is that BHPH financing universally carries high interest rates and rigid payment schedules, and this dealership does not disclose its terms online — total cost of ownership will be significantly higher than a conventional auto loan.
services: Buy Here Pay Here in-house vehicle financing
Used car, truck, and SUV sales
In-house financing with no credit score requirement
Vehicle trade-ins and outright car purchases
Online payment portal for loan payments
Two-location dealership network (Conyers, GA and Griffin, GA)
